the assessments are aligned with the code's purpose.\n\n3. **Scores Given to Each Issue**:\n   - Malware scores are all 0, which is appropriate given the absence of malicious activity.\n   - Security risk scores are low (mostly 0.1), reflecting minimal risk associated with static interface code.\n   - The scores seem reasonable and consistent with the code's nature.\n\n4. **Justification for Risk Scores > 0.5**:\n   - None of the reports assign a risk higher than 0.5, which is appropriate.\n   - The minimal risk scores reflect the code's role as a read-only interface wrapper with no active or malicious behavior.\n\n5. **Malware Score Reflection**:\n   - All reports correctly assign malware scores of 0, indicating no malicious activity detected.\n   - The code does not perform any suspicious operations, data exfiltration, or code injection.\n\n6. **Claims Based on Assumptions**:\n   - The reports avoid unwarranted assumptions; The security and malware scores are appropriately low.**\n\n### My Final Estimation:\n\n- **Malware Score**: 0 (no malicious activity detected)\n- **Obfuscated Score**: 0 (no obfuscation present)\n- **Risk Score**: 0.1 (minimal, inherent to static interface code, not executable or active code)\n\n**Rationale**: The code is purely a boilerplate, static ABI wrapper generated for safe blockchain interactions.
